assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Tasks 1-3 target trustify-backend (e.g., task-1-backend-comparison-model.md: '## Repository\ntrustify-backend'). Tasks 4-6 target trustify-ui (e.g., task-4-frontend-comparison-api.md: '## Repository\ntrustify-ui'). summary-comment.md confirms '3 backend, 3 frontend'.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are numbered 1, 2, 3. Frontend tasks are numbered 4, 5, 6. Task 4 (frontend) depends on Task 3 (backend) per its Dependencies section: 'Depends on: Task 3 — Backend SBOM comparison endpoint and integration tests'. All backend task numbers (1-3) are lower than all frontend task numbers (4-6). impact-map.md dependency graph confirms: Task 3 -> Task 4 is the cross-repo edge.

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 5 (SbomComparePage, UI-facing) extensively references Figma design context and specific PatternFly components: 'Use the Figma design context for the full-page layout', 'PatternFly Select components (single, typeahead variant)', 'PatternFly Button', 'PatternFly Dropdown', 'PatternFly ExpandableSection', 'PatternFly Badge', 'PatternFly Table (composable variant)', 'PatternFly EmptyState', 'PatternFly Skeleton', 'CodeBranchIcon'. Task 6 (route/list integration, UI-facing) references 'PatternFly Table row selection using the composable table's select feature', 'PatternFly Button labeled Compare selected'. Task 4 (API layer) is exempt and does not reference Figma design, which is correct.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 4 (frontend, trustify-ui) explicitly depends on Task 3 (backend, trustify-backend) in its Dependencies section: 'Depends on: Task 3 — Backend SBOM comparison endpoint and integration tests'. This is a cross-repo dependency from frontend to backend. impact-map.md confirms: 'Task 3 (backend endpoint + tests) -> Task 4 (frontend API layer) [cross-repo dependency]'.

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md: Task 1 modifies 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs' (in repo-backend.md), references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs' and 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs'. Task 2 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/error.rs'. Task 3 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs', 'tests/api/sbom.rs'. Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md: Task 4 modifies 'src/api/models.ts', 'src/api/rest.ts', references 'src/hooks/useSbomById.ts', 'src/api/client.ts'. Task 5 references 'src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx', 'src/pages/SbomDetailPage/components/PackageTable.tsx'. Task 6 modifies 'src/routes.tsx', 'src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx', references 'tests/e2e/sbom-list.spec.ts'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have a '## Repository' section with exactly one repo name: Tasks 1-3 have 'trustify-backend', Tasks 4-6 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files contain the required sections: '## Target Branch' (all set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1-backend-comparison-model.md, task-2-backend-comparison-service.md, task-3-backend-comparison-endpoint.md, task-4-frontend-comparison-api.md, task-5-frontend-comparison-page.md, task-6-frontend-route-and-list-integration.md.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Files to Modify paths reference existing manifest paths: Task 1 modifies 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs' (in repo-backend.md line 41). Task 2 modifies 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs' (line 46). Task 3 modifies 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs' (line 49). Task 4 modifies 'src/api/models.ts' (repo-frontend.md line 25) and 'src/api/rest.ts' (line 26). Task 5 creates no files in unmapped directories. Task 6 modifies 'src/routes.tsx' (line 53) and 'src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx' (line 33). Files to Create are new files in existing manifest directories (e.g., 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs' in the existing model/ directory, 'src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts' in the existing hooks/ directory).

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend, non-documentation task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) and documentation tasks are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks (none are bookend or documentation tasks) have Implementation Notes with specific file paths and code patterns. Task 1: 'Follow the existing model pattern in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs and modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/details.rs. Each struct should derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and Debug.' Task 2: 'Follow the service pattern in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs... Use SbomService... Query packages using PackageService from modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs.' Task 3: 'Follow the endpoint pattern in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs and list.rs... use assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK) pattern.' Task 4: 'follow the pattern of existing functions like fetchSboms(). Use the Axios client from src/api/client.ts... follow the pattern in src/hooks/useSbomById.ts.' Task 5: references src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx with specific usage details. Task 6: 'Follow the existing lazy-loading pattern in src/routes.tsx... reference the existing FilterToolbar component at src/components/FilterToolbar.tsx.'

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have '## Target Branch\nmain', consistent with direct-to-main workflow. impact-map.md confirms: 'Direct-to-main — All tasks target main branch.'

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have separate digest files with valid SHA-256 hashes: task-1-digest.md: 'sha256-md:4cca81481400ef81c257823166c424a3b6c57cf8ab3d70faffc33be7a0fdbd0c' (64 hex chars), task-2-digest.md: 'sha256-md:af79a99b2f747eb88ce5d1fa54530687b92f90284b6a8a3a9553b57a7d17b72e', task-3-digest.md: 'sha256-md:509bcc39d932705b8b6a120489ee1875a827ed3e11de796c043e51b8f317c4a8', task-4-digest.md: 'sha256-md:fb703fa6128b34328be0b68b64cecaf344bef97fa2b0834b6b51f412df89482b', task-5-digest.md: 'sha256-md:ea9cea89991fce3d7a9cb006f7ffb89dd1d46f86c0938e6542a0590ed2e99ee4', task-6-digest.md: 'sha256-md:302fde6f22b0722fc480319080508af541476a52efcec16bb5cdbd7c2dc14980'. All use 'sha256-md:' prefix with exactly 64 lowercase hex characters.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No evidence of convention-aware enrichment in any output file. Searched all task descriptions for 'Per CONVENTIONS.md', 'Applies:', or convention applicability rationale — none found. The only references to 'convention' are generic (task-1: 'Reference for struct conventions and serde derivations', task-2: 'per the error handling convention'). These are informal prose references, not the prescribed format from convention-applicability-rules.md ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'). No convention applicability validation is evidenced.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Field Inheritance table shows 'Priority | Critical | TC-9003' and states 'All tasks inherit Priority: Critical and Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 from the parent feature TC-9003.' summary-comment.md Inherited Field Propagation section shows 'Priority | Critical' and states 'All 6 tasks inherit the following fields from feature TC-9003'.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: The Feature has fixVersions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (non-empty). No '### Jira Field Defaults' section exists in CLAUDE.md, so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both'. impact-map.md Field Inheritance table shows 'Fix Versions | RHTPA 1.5.0 | TC-9003' and confirms all tasks inherit it. summary-comment.md Inherited Field Propagation table shows 'Fix Versions | RHTPA 1.5.0' with 'All 6 tasks inherit the following fields from feature TC-9003'.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in the outputs directory and contains an '## Inherited Field Propagation' section stating 'All 6 tasks inherit the following fields from feature TC-9003' with a table showing Priority: Critical and Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0. Additionally, impact-map.md contains a '## Field Inheritance' section with the same information.
